title: Listening - Part 3
description: For each question, write the correct answer in the gap. Write one or two words or a number or a date or a time.
instructions: You will hear one person giving information. For questions 14–19, write the correct answer in the gap.
context: You will hear an organiser talking to volunteers at a music festival.
audio_script: Speaker: Hello, everyone, and thank you for offering to help at the weekend music festival. We need volunteers to make sure visitors can find the stages, buy tickets and enjoy the day safely. When you arrive on Saturday, please go first to the volunteer tent. It is next to the [14]ticket office[/14], near the main entrance. You will collect your badge there. The first band starts at eleven, but volunteers should arrive at [15]eight forty-five[/15] so we have time to explain the jobs. Most of you will help visitors, but some people will work in the children's area. If you are there, your job is to help with [16]face painting[/16] and simple games. You do not need experience, because a team leader will show you what to do. If a visitor asks you a question and you do not know the answer, take them to the information desk. Please be friendly, but do not leave your area unless your team leader says it is all right. Lunch is provided. You can collect a sandwich and a drink from the [17]blue van[/17] behind the main stage. Please take your break before two o'clock, as the afternoon will be very busy. The festival finishes at seven, but we need help putting chairs away afterwards. That should take about [18]thirty[/18] minutes.  At the end of the day, please return your radio to the volunteer tent. If it rains, we will still continue, so bring a coat. The emergency phone number is printed on the back of your [19]badge[/19]. Please keep it with you all day.

items.text_before: Volunteer tent is next to the:

questions.prompt: Volunteer tent is next to the:
questions.explanation: The volunteer tent is next to the ticket office.
Volunteers should arrive at eight forty-five.
Volunteers in the children's area help with face painting and games.
Lunch can be collected from the blue van.
Putting chairs away should take about thirty minutes.
The emergency number is printed on the back of the badge.
